column1: GENIE LOCATION ACCURACY  --  FULL STORY
Cohort: Delhi, Dec 2025. Non-BDO filter applied.
Status: Analysis complete. Final answers in sections 11-14.
OPERATIONAL CONTEXT (anchor for new readers):
Allocation runs BEFORE partner decisions. It ranks candidate partners by install
likelihood; the top-ranked partner is notified first. Production comparison of
signals is therefore UNCONDITIONAL -- the state a new booking arrives in.
The 'conditional-accept' slice we compute in section 9 is a DIAGNOSTIC only,
not an operational comparison. Details in section 14.
## 1. THE QUESTION
Does the partner-to-booking distance at allocation predict installation outcomes,
and which decline reasons cluster where?
SCOPE: this is a post-Promise-Maker analysis. Every row already passed the 25m
serviceability gate (booking_location CTE filters to serviceable = TRUE). We are
evaluating ALLOCATION (GNN ranking of partners for a booking) -- NOT Promise Maker.
A separate lead-level Promise Maker analysis will follow.
Why it matters: Allocation keys on nearest_distance to rank partners. If the signal
is confounded with partner tenure (splitter vs active_base) or with decline-reason
gaming (area -> address_not_clear substitution), every ranking inherits the bias.
## 2. COHORT & FILTERS
Source: Delhi, Dec 2025 bookings. Query: query_unified_correl.txt.
Grain: one row per (mobile, partner_id) pair. rn=1 on first decision (ORDER BY added_time ASC).
Decision events considered: INTERESTED, ASSIGNED, DECLINED.
Exclusion: DECLINED with source='System_Force_Declined_Post_Assigned_72hours' (system auto-decline).
Python-side filter: bdo_lead == 0 (non-BDO bookings only).
Rationale: BDO bookings are ops-captured -- different journey from self-serve.

column2: Distance deciles on install cohort (INTERESTED/ASSIGNED). Install rate: 72.5% -> 22.6%. Distance matters.
Distance deciles on decline cohort. Excluded System_Force_Declined_Post_Assigned_72hours. Built regex for area-related declines.

Read: install rate drops ~50% -> ~7%. Monotonic decay.
Both decline buckets rise monotonically. Area ~6% -> ~26%, address ~10% -> ~29%.
Decile 10 d_max is a long tail -- likely data hygiene (partner lat/lng errors).
## 6. INSTALL / DECLINES BY PROBABILITY DECILE (non-BDO)
Bucket: pd.qcut on GNN probability. Decile 1 = lowest predicted prob, decile 10 = highest.
probability NULL in ~7% of rows -- these drop from this slice but stay in slices 5, 7.
prob_decile
Separations:
metric
pct_installed
pct_area_decline
pct_address_not_clear
Read: probability dominates raw distance on every metric.
At prob_decile 1, ~48% of allocations end in 'couldn't understand address'. When the GNN
thinks the match is poor, partners dismiss via address_not_clear almost half the time.
Direct numerical support for the dismissal-leak hypothesis.
## 7. NEAREST_TYPE GROUPBY (active_base vs splitter)
Partners with no prior install have no active_base point -- the system uses 'splitter'
(a fixed distribution point) as the install-proxy. Splitter is the cold-start signal.
nearest_type
active_base
splitter
Read: splitter share ~30%. Install rate ~22% vs ~37% for active_base (-15pp).
Both decline buckets ~6pp higher for splitter. Splitter d_median is higher (~25 vs ~18 km) --
raw gap partly distance-driven. Matched-distance contrast is the cleaner next step.
## 8. PROB DECILE × NEAREST_TYPE  --  does GNN already encode type?
If GNN learned from the splitter edges Maanas fed it, two things should hold:
(a) It should route splitters to lower prob deciles (composition shifts).
(b) Within a fixed prob decile, active_base and splitter should install at similar rates.
8a. Composition -- % splitter per prob decile:
prob_decile
Read: monotonic decline 38% -> 12% splitter share. GNN DOES route splitters to low prob. (a) PASSES.
8b. Side-by-side rates per (prob_decile, nearest_type):
prob_decile
Read: at every prob decile, splitter installs LESS than active_base. Gap collapses at extremes
(decile 1: ~3pp, decile 10: ~3pp) but is 10-17pp at mid-prob (deciles 3-6). (b) PARTIAL PASS.
Interpretation (two non-exclusive reads):
1. Model-side: GNN has less signal on splitter edges than decision edges -- noisier in the middle.
2. Partner-side: splitters face real-world friction (no routing experience, no local infra)
that doesn't show up in any graph edge -- genuine meatspace cold-start.
Matched-distance 2km bins within fixed prob decile would split these.
## 9. DIAGNOSTIC SLICE  --  conditional-accept (decomposition, NOT operational comparison)
CAVEAT before reading: this is a diagnostic decomposition, not an operational comparison.
Allocation runs BEFORE partner decisions (see section 14). This slice tells us WHERE
probability's unconditional edge over distance comes from -- it does NOT tell us what
Allocation should use as its ranking signal.
Slice: filter to decision_event IN ('INTERESTED','ASSIGNED'), then install_rate by decile.
Purpose: separate the partner-willingness leg from the install-physics leg.
cohort
Separation comparison (D1 -> D10):
slice
unconditional (all decisions -- production-relevant)
conditional-accept (diagnostic only)
Two reads from this slice:
a. Physical ceiling at the tail: at D10 conditional-install is 22.79%. Even accepted
bookings fail 3 of 4 times at ~74km. This is Wiom's fiber-reach Shannon limit.
Informs: treat 50km+ as a tail refusal zone (action #2 in recommendations).
b. Decomposition of prob's advantage: prob carries install-physics signal AND partner-
willingness signal. Conditional-accept strips the willingness channel, leaving just
physics. Both axes carry similar physics signal (~40% sep). Prob's unconditional
57% vs distance's 44% = the ~13pp willingness-channel contribution.
Do NOT read this as 'probability is really just a decline predictor'. The willingness
channel is a real, valid signal that Allocation needs at inference time -- partners
haven't declined yet, but the GNN knows from history who usually does. That's the
information distance can't encode.
Earlier rounds of analysis misread this slice as a critique. That reading was retracted
in round 6 (see section 14 + timeline).
## 10. TENURE-GAP INVESTIGATION  --  what drives the splitter-vs-active_base install gap?
Hypotheses tested against partner-level data from t_partner + t_active_base + t_node_splitter_gs:
H1 -- Cold-start: gap comes from newer partners who haven't built active_base yet.
H2 -- Splitter-share: gap comes from partners who roam-submit lat/lng as splitter points to
attract bookings. Gaming-adjacent behavior unseen by the GNN.
10a. Install rate by tenure_bucket x nearest_type (H1 test):
tenure
Read: gap persists (18pp) for 730d+ mature partners. Tenure does NOT explain the gap. H1 REJECTED.
10b. Install rate by prob_decile x partner_splitter_share (H2 test):
splitter_share = splitter_count / (splitter_count + active_base_count)  [dedup'd lat/lng]
prob_decile
Read: at prob_decile 4, partners with <10% splitter share install at 35.77% vs 5.00% for partners
with >90% splitter share. 31pp gap at the SAME probability. H2 SUPPORTED. Gaming-adjacent partners
get mid-prob rankings they don't deserve based on actual install performance. GNN does not currently
discount this. High prob deciles have ~0 high-splitter-share partners -- GNN correctly filters the
extreme tail, but the middle is where the leak sits.
Note: t_node_splitter_gs already stored as distinct (partner, lat, lng) -- 0 duplicate rows.
Gaming pattern = many *different* lat/lngs per partner, not repeated pings of one point.
## 11. KEY FINDINGS  --  full stack

column2: Distance predicts install rate: ~50% -> ~7% across non-BDO deciles. Unconditional separation 44%.

Probability outperforms distance unconditionally (57% sep) but COLLAPSES to match distance when we condition on accept (40% vs 40%). Probability was modeling decline-likelihood, not physical install probability.
At distance D10, conditional install rate is 22.79% (even accepted bookings fail 3 of 4 times). Physical ceiling at the tail.
Address_not_clear is a dismissal channel, not pure parsing failure. 48% at prob_decile 1, 2.5% at decile 10.
The 10-17pp splitter vs active_base install gap is NOT tenure-driven. 730d+ partners show 18pp gap. H1 rejected.
The gap IS partner splitter_share driven. At prob_decile 4: 35.77% install (share<10%) vs 5.00% install (share>90%). 31pp gap at same GNN probability. H2 supported.
Non-BDO is 43.5% of total volume but has SHALLOWER distance gradient -- BDO was driving some of the raw signal.
Decile 10 tail is data hygiene -- isolate as separate population before trusting as a decile.

column1: ## 13. REVISED READ  --  HOW TO LOOK AT THE DATA NOW (Geoff + Donna round 3)
Both agents independently converged on a re-factoring of how we should think about Allocation.
From Geoff (first-principles):
P(install) = P(accept) * P(install | accept)
Distance predicts the SECOND term (physics: cable reach, survey fatigue, travel).
Probability predicts the PRODUCT but mostly loads on the FIRST term (partner willingness).
When you condition on accept, you kill term 1 -- prob and distance both collapse to term 2,
which is geometric. The GNN's apparent dominance over distance was compositional. The D10
conditional install rate of 22.79% is Wiom's fiber-reach Shannon limit -- no ranking punches through.
From Donna (systems):
Splitter submission is a reinforcing 'success to the successful' loop (Meadows #3):
more splitters submitted -> wider notification footprint -> more allocations
-> occasional installs -> partner perceives gaming as rational -> more splitters
No balancing loop exists because splitter points never expire or get validated against installs.
The stock being gamed is PERCEIVED SERVICEABILITY.
Convergent inferences:

column2: Allocation objective should factorize: (a) accept model = P(accept|notification), (b) conditional-install model = P(install|accept). Rank on the product but inspect each leg.
The current GNN 'probability' is a decline-likelihood predictor wearing fit-predictor clothes. Distance is an orthogonal geometric signal governing the physics leg.

Adding splitter_share as a GNN feature is a band-aid. Gamers will mutate the pattern (e.g. keep share below threshold via multiple partner IDs). Durable fix: validate splitter points against install outcomes -- turn unbounded stock into one with a decay flow.
The training label itself is contaminated -- gamers generate splitter points AND fail to install. Every retrain re-inherits the pathology. Real fix is UPSTREAM, before nearest-distance computation.

Geoff's architectural question: does Allocation need a REFUSAL PRIMITIVE? A way to return a booking to Promise Maker saying 'no partner I rank can clear the physics bar.' Today every booking routes; losses absorb as declines.
D10 is a hard physical ceiling (~23% conditional install). Any intervention at D10 is about routing away, not improving. Needs to be disambiguated from data-hygiene tail (448km+).

column1: Distance is a GAMEABLE axis: partners submit their own splitter lat/lngs, which
directly affect the nearest_distance they see. GNN integrates DECLINE HISTORY that
gamers cannot fake, creating an active balancing loop on gaming behavior.
Why GNN wins: it learns from decline signals, distance doesn't.
A distance-based approach is a fixed geometric cut -- it cannot adapt to partner
behavior. It does not price decline signals partners send. A partner who declines
80% of allocations looks identical to one who installs 80%, if they're at the same
distance. The GNN integrates both install and decline edge types (per Maanas's
design), weighted geographically and temporally. The GNN's advantage IS the
decline-signal channel. Any signal that doesn't learn from declines gives this up.
Diagnostic slice -- conditional-accept (NOT production-relevant):
When we filter to decision_event IN ('INTERESTED','ASSIGNED'), the install-rate
separations become: distance 40%, probability 40%. They tie. But this is not what
Allocation sees at inference time -- Allocation ranks BEFORE any decision is known.
The conditional-accept collapse is just the decline signal being removed from the
data, which is precisely what GNN is designed to price. Earlier rounds of analysis
(and two agent rounds) over-read this as a critique. Retracted.

column1: Address-not-clear
The address-not-clear monotonic rise IS the dismissal-leak signature -- partners
farther out use 'address unclear' as a polite decline regardless of actual address
clarity. If it were pure parsing failure, the curve would be flat on distance.
## 15. GNN CONTEXT  --  side note (parked, not load-bearing)
This analysis was NOT about GNN architecture. The data answers above stand on their
own. This section is context for future GNN work only.
Design intent (per Maanas): for every target booking, build a graph that looks at
historical install and decline decisions by a partner, across partners, along with
splitter data. Decisions + splitters become edge types, geographically + temporally
separated. The GNN then predicts whether a target (booking, partner) match will install,
based on how the partner has behaved across actions (install or decline) in space and time.
One detail worth flagging for future GNN work, surfaced during analysis:
- create_gnn.py computes a precomputed attention bias per edge:
attention = exp(-time) * 1/(1+dist) * (1 - DECLINED) * (1 + INSTALLED)
and stores it as the last column of edge_attr.
- The GATConv instances are initialized WITHOUT edge_dim.
- In PyTorch Geometric, edge_attr is consumed by GATConv only when edge_dim is set
at init (which builds lin_edge). Without edge_dim, edge_attr is silently dropped
(older PyG) or raises (newer PyG).
- Likely implication: the precomputed attention formula is ORPHAN at runtime --
computed and stored but not consumed. Message passing uses GATConv's own learned
attention over node features + topology. Both install and decline edge types
contribute first-class messages, exactly as intended.
- If confirmed at execution: either the HTML doc's attention-interaction claim
needs revision, or the code should pass edge_dim=1 to activate the precomputed bias.
Earlier rounds of Geoff + Donna review (round 4) built an 'architectural critique'
(decline-complement, survivorship bias, three structural fixes) on the documented
attention formula. That critique was retracted in round 5 when the code was read.
Logged here for completeness; not load-bearing on this analysis.
